title: Xenial Winey Filipino Noodles Pancit Canton
description: A winey and romantic Filipino dish featuring rosemary and salt-bake flavors, served with glassy-jelly noodles, this pancit canton is a unique twist on a classic recipe, with a focus on raw-friendly ingredients and a delicate balance of flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the salt and baking soda.
Rub the mixture all over the rosemary sprigs and place them on a baking sheet.
Bake the rosemary in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until fragrant.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the white wine to the pan and bring to a simmer.
Let the wine reduce by half, stirring occasionally, about 5 minutes.
Add the pancit canton noodles to the pan and toss to coat with the wine sauce.
